howItWorks.intro: This craft pricing calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Pay yourself at least $15-25/hr for labor
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How do I price handmade items?
faq.answer: Formula: (Materials + Labor + Overhead) × Markup = Wholesale price. Double wholesale for retail. Don't undervalue your time — pay yourself at least $15-25/hr. If the price seems high, you may need cheaper materials or faster processes.

faq.question: What is keystone pricing?
faq.answer: Keystone is doubling the wholesale price to get retail. If your wholesale is $30, retail is $60. This accounts for marketplace fees, shipping materials, returns, and profit. Etsy/craft fairs typically work at retail pricing.

faq.question: Why are my products not selling at the 'right' price?
faq.answer: Common issues: product photos don't convey quality, wrong marketplace for the price point, not enough perceived value, or too much competition. Consider: better photography, targeting higher-end markets, or adding value through packaging and branding.

faq.question: Is the Craft Pricing Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard crafting and DIY formulas trusted by crafters and makers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Craft Pricing Calculator?
sections.body: The Craft Pricing Calculator is a free online tool designed for crafters and makers who need quick, accurate calculations in the crafting and DIY space. By entering your material cost, time to make, your hourly rate, you get instant results including wholesale price, retail price, effective hourly earnings. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting wholesale price right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In crafting and DIY, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by crafters and makers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your material cost and need to find the right wholesale price. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Material Cost ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your material cost — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: time to make, your hourly rate, profit markup. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your wholesale price, retail price, effective hourly earnings. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your material cost before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both wholesale price and retail price — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to plan your projects.
If you're unsure about your profit markup, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
